                                                                                           |            |
|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| SDG                                                                                                | Sub-target | Aspect/issue                                                                                    | Lufthansa Group's contribution to achieving the global SDGs as defined in the 2030 Agenda of the United Nations                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | Page                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |            |
|                                                                                                    | 4.5        | Employee concerns/<br>Transformation capabilities                                               | Multiple changes call for long-term transformation process and continuous transformation support:<br>Employees are offered regular professional training courses in order to continuously update their skills and abilities. In 2022, a department was<br>established with the task of conducting a structured evaluation of the impacts on future roles and competence profiles of employees in all pro-<br>fessional groups and to accompany them through changes. In the reporting year, this approach was applied to the administrative employees of<br>Deutsche Lufthansa AG for the first time. The findings will be recorded in 2024, together with those from the participating departments,<br>and measures will be identified on this basis. In this context, the Lufthansa Group has established a supportive process that enables employees<br>to analyse the future viability of their own skill sets and to compare them with the current and future requirements for their roles. This is combined<br>with wide-ranging opportunities for self-directed learning. Besides the traditional Group-wide training portfolio offered by the Lufthansa Group<br>Campus, all employees in the Lufthansa Group have access to over 12,000 digital video courses in German and English, enabling self-directed learning.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   | → p. 104f.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |            |
| <br>Image: AFFORDABLE AND CLEAN ENERGY<br>                                                         | 7.2        | Environmental concerns/<br>Climate protection                                                   | In the past decade, the Lufthansa Group has paid close attention to research into SAF (i.e. synthetic kerosene produced from renewable energy<br>sources) and its testing and use. Since this time, the Lufthansa Group has worked in partnerships to drive key technologies for SAF production.<br>Through letters of intent the Lufthansa Group secures access to required SAF volumes.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        | → p.80                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |            |
|                                                                                                    |            |                                                                                                 | Sustainable aviation fuel to be used more widely:<br>SAF sales are set to be increased via the continuous development of more sustainable products, which customers can use throughout the travel<br>chain, e.g. during the booking process or in-flight.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        | → p. 82                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |            |
|                                                                                                    |            |                                                                                                 | To enable the continuous procurement of SAF on the spot market, up to USD 250 million has been released for the period up to 2026 by the<br>Executive Board of Deutsche Lufthansa AG. In addition, options for long-term supply commitments around the world are under consideration.<br>These are expected to offer considerable production volumes and security of supply from around 2025 onwards. 2023, two further letters of<br>intent were signed in order to secure a permanent supply of sustainable aviation fuels for the Lufthansa Group. In the long term, the Lufthansa<br>Group will provide support for innovative supply concepts with the goal of transforming today's start-ups and developers into tomorrow's suppli-<br>ers. In this regard, the Lufthansa Group focuses on synthetic kerosene based on residual materials, ligneous biomass and renewable electrical<br>energy (power-to-liquid – PtL) as well as on research into the direct use of sunlight for fuel synthesis. To accelerate the market introduction of<br>particularly forward-looking sustainable aviation fuels, the Lufthansa Group intends to establish an SAF Buyers Alliance next year. This is intended<br>to pool demand on the part of multiple corporate customers, to enable the construction of pilot systems for fuel generation.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         | → p. 82                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |            |
|                                                                                                    |            |                                                                                                 | In 2023, Lufthansa German Airlines signed a letter of intent for a research partnership with the German Aerospace Center, Munich Airport, Airbus<br>and MTU for operational testing of every aspect of PtL use in daily flight operations. This includes a wide range of questions relating to the use of<br>SAF, such as the use of pure (100%) PtL fuel and the effect of SAF on the formation of contrails.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   | → p.83                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |            |
|                                                                                                    |            |                                                                                                 | At the political level, the Lufthansa Group supports a global strategy for the supply of sustainable aviation fuels, such as through its membership<br>of the ICAO Fuel Task Group since 2023. At the level of the EU, the Lufthansa Group advocates a simplification of the compliance processes for<br>SAF-related regulation and has prepared a position paper on a book and claim system. This involves trading SAF on the basis of certificates rather<br>than physically.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | p. 83                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |            |
| SDG                                                                                                | Sub-target | Aspect/issue                                                                                    | Lufthansa Group's contribution to achieving the global SDGs as defined in the 2030 Agenda of the United Nations | 8<br><span style="font-size: 8px;">DECENT WORK AND<br/>ECONOMIC GROWTH</span><br>Image: [SDG Icon] | 8.2        | About this combined non-financial declaration                                                   | Value-based management is an integral element of sustainable corporate governance for the Lufthansa Group. Sustainability considerations are increasingly helping to ensure financial resilience. The financial strategy of the Lufthansa Group seeks to increase its Company value in a sustainable manner. |                                                                                                    |            |                                                                                                 | The impact of the Company on non-financial aspects is taken into account in the Group risk management system of the Lufthansa Group.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |            |
|                                                                                                    |            | Employee concerns/<br>Transformation capabilities                                               | Hybrid event formats support employees' ongoing development                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | → p.10                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |            |
|                                                                                                    |            |                                                                                                 | Mobility and flexibility are key building blocks for modern working environments. By the end of 2023, all Lufthansa Group employees had access to cloud-based software. In addition to mobile devices, this software is an important prerequisite for remote and flexible working.<br>The Lufthansa Group helps its employees to act sustainably in relation to environmental and social issues in their fields of work. In 2023, the Lufthansa Group once again ran its Green Explorer learning and engagement programme for 150 selected above-average performers, the aim being to transform them into multipliers and driving forces for promoting sustainability in daily business operations.<br>Service Management Professional apprenticeship continues: 2023, 336 employees successfully completed this skill-building programme. This means that 1,830 employees have successfully completed this training since the programme was first introduced.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   | → p.10                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |            |
|                                                                                                    | 8.5        | Employee concerns/<br>Attractiveness as an employer                                             | Uniform performance and potential assessment enable that talent is retained.<br>Key remuneration components and additional benefits are offered.<br>Employer value proposition (EVP) is introduced to enhance the Group's attractiveness as an employer. In the context of the EVP, the updated employer brand "Lufthansa Group careers" was launched. This was accompanied by three major marketing campaigns, which supported the successful recruitment of more than 14,890 employees in 2023.<br>Working and employment conditions for employees and managers are flexible and are adapted continually<br>The Engagement Index provides information about the Company's attractiveness as an employer and has been tracked by the voluntary annual employee survey involve me! since 2015. In 2023, the Engagement Index value was 2.2 and increased by 0.2 compared with the previous year's level of 2.4.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | → p.10<br>→ p.99<br>→ p.10<br>→ p.99<br>→ p.10                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |            |
|                                                                                                    | 8.7        | Business ethics and compliance/<br>Respect for human rights                                     | Respect for human rights is a key issue for the Lufthansa Group. As an employer, the Lufthansa Group directly contributes to the livelihoods of more than 96,677 employees and their families worldwide. Its value chain also includes a large number of business partners whose employees contribute indirectly to the Lufthansa Group's business success and may be dependent on it. This means that the Lufthansa Group shares responsibility for the concerns of its own workforce and, indirectly, for those of the employees along its value chain too. This is also reflected in working conditions at the Group companies, the guarantee of freedom of association and assembly, rules to ensure gender equality and the inclusion of minorities. It is also demonstrated in the expectation expressed in the Supplier Code of Conduct that the business partners included in the value chain should also adhere to these same principles.<br>In 2023, the Executive Board of Deutsche Lufthansa AG approved a policy statement on the human rights strategy of the Lufthansa Group. This policy statement reflects the Company's commitment to ensuring respect for human rights. It describes how the Lufthansa Group fulfils its due diligence requirements, which high-priority human rights- and environment-related risks have been identified on the basis of the risk analysis and which human rights- and environment-related expectations apply to the employees of the Lufthansa Group and its suppliers. This is supplemented by the Lufthansa Group's Code of Conduct, which applies to all board members, managers and employees of the Lufthansa Group, and its Supplier Code of Conduct. These two codes of conduct were revised in 2023 and now also reflect the requirements of the German Corporate Due Diligence in Supply Chains Act (LkSG).<br>The Lufthansa Group updates management approach for addressing human trafficking<br>Mandatory trainings on human rights, diversity and inclusion established Information formats and campaigns for employees to be conducted<br>Dialogue formats support the development of knowledge on corporate responsibility<br>Processes for reporting human rights violations implemented<br>Human rights, and environment-related risk analysis implemented | → p.113<br>→ p.11<br>→ p.11<br>→ p.115<br>→ p.114<br>→ p.116                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |            |

Human rights- and environment-related obligations are part of supplier contracts |                                                                                                    |            | Employee concerns/<br>Attractiveness as an employer                                             | Unrestricted freedom of association is an established feature of the Lufthansa Group.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |            |
|                                                                                                    |            |                                                                                                 | Labour union partners are firmly established at the Lufthansa Group:<br>As of 31 December 2023, of the 63,176 Lufthansa Group employees in Germany, 76% were covered directly by collective bargaining arrangements.<br>The remaining 24% are largely managers and non-payscale employees in senior positions. However, many of the arrangements for these non-payscale<br>employees are also based on the collective bargaining agreements. In the Europe region, 98% of employees at Austrian Airlines and 87% of those at<br>SWISS have an employment contract covered by collective agreements. |                                                                                                    |            | Employee concerns/<br>Health and safety at work                                                 | See SDGs 3.3, 3.4 and 3.8 | <br>Image: Industry, innovation and infrastructure icon                                            | 9.4        | Environmental concerns/Climate<br>protection/Research and innovation                            | Fleet renewal is continuously driven forward:<br>The Lufthansa Group fleet was expanded by 29 new aircraft in 2023, including Airbus A320neos, A321neos, A350-900s and Boeing 787-9s,<br>which are powered by modern engines. For example, the A320neo aircraft is one of the most modern and most environmentally friendly aircraft in<br>the world and is much quieter than comparable aircraft types. Since 2019, the Lufthansa Group has more than doubled the share of latest technol-<br>ogy in its fleet to 20% (141 aircraft). A total of 18 older aircraft have been removed from the Group fleet in exchange. In December 2023, a total of<br>80 additional Boeing 737 MAX and Airbus A220-300 short- and medium-haul aircraft were ordered and 120 purchase options were signed.<br>Together with previous orders, the order book now comprises around 260 latest-generation aircraft.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |            |
|                                                                                                    |            |                                                                                                 | The Hydrogen Aviation Lab is testing maintenance and ground processes for future aircraft generations with hydrogen as a primary energy source                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |            |
|                                                                                                    |            |                                                                                                 | Fleet renewal is continuously driven forward<br>The AeroSHARK surface coating developed jointly by Lufthansa Technik and BASF, which is named after the microscopic ribbing modelled on<br>shark skin, reduces aircraft air resistance. At the end of 2023, the Lufthansa Group had a total of 15 Boeing 777s fitted with AeroSHARK in service,<br>including 11 Boeing 777-300ERs at SWISS and 4 Boeing 777Fs at Lufthansa Cargo. Following the modification of all Group aircraft of these two<br>types, cumulative savings in excess of 8,000 tonnes of fuel and more than 25,000 tonnes of CO2 per year are expected in the future. | T201 SUSTAINABLE DEVELOPMENT GOALS (SDG)                                                           |            |                                                                                                 | | SDG                                                                                                | Sub-target | Aspect/issue                                                                                    | Lufthansa Group's contribution to achieving the global SDGs as defined in the 2030 Agenda of the United Nations                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | Page                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |            |
|                                                                                                    | 9.5        | Environmental concerns/Climate protec-<br>tion/Research and innovation                          | Flight operations management optimised thanks to artificial intelligence:<br>Together with Google Cloud, the Lufthansa Group has developed the Operations Decision Support Suite (OPSD) management tool. This in turn<br>can help to avoid carbon emissions, for instance by ensuring that the aircraft with the optimum fuel-saving profile is selected for a specific route<br>or that seat occupancy is optimised. It was introduced in 2023 at SWISS, as the first airline in the Lufthansa Group.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           | p.84                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |            |
|                                                                                                    |            |                                                                                                 | More efficient concepts for departure and approach are being implemented and progress made with the digitalisation of approach technologies:<br>Modern, satellite-based Required Navigation Performance (RNP) technology plays an important role in the introduction of new flight procedures<br>at European airports. With this technology, the International Civil Aviation Organization (ICAO) has set minimum standards for the navigation<br>performance required of an aircraft. A shorter RNP departure route newly introduced at Stuttgart Airport in 2023 is used by Eurowings aircraft,<br>saving around 26 tonnes of fuel and thus 81 tonnes of CO₂ in 2023. Austrian Airlines also uses the fuel-saving RNP approach procedure at<br>Vienna Airport. This procedure also affects noise emissions.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    | p.84                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |            |
|                                                                                                    |            |                                                                                                 | The Lufthansa Group has been involved in various research projects examining the atmosphere and the climate since 1994. As part of the European<br>research infrastructure I-AGOS (In-service Aircraft for a Global Observing System), the Lufthansa Group works closely with its research partners<br>from the Karlsruhe Institute of Technology and the Jülich Research Centre to equip selected passenger aircraft with measuring instruments that<br>collect data about the condition of the atmosphere on scheduled flights. This data is freely accessible and is used by around 300 organisations<br>worldwide. It helps scientists to acquire new knowledge about climate change and the atmosphere and to detect long-term changes. Since 2021,<br>Lufthansa Airlines' Airbus A350 "Erfurt" has been undergoing conversion for use as the new platform for the IAGOS-CARIBIC (Civil Aircraft for<br>the Regular Investigation of the Atmosphere Based on an Instrument Container) measurement system. The second key project milestone was achieved<br>in 2023. The newly developed measurement probe system was installed in an aircraft for the first time in order to complete a flight programme<br>agreed with the certification authority. As a final step, the new measuring equipment container is to be developed and approved for use by exter-<br>nal research partners of the Lufthansa Group. A maiden flight with the overall system is due to take place at the end of 2024.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | p.85f.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |            |
|                                                                                                    |            |                                                                                                 | The Lufthansa Group is examining measures to reduce its overall climate impact:<br>At Germany's National Aviation Conference in September 2023, the German airlines announced test flights aimed at avoiding contrails which<br>impact the climate. Lufthansa Airlines will take part in these flights as part of the D-KULT research project, which has been under way since 2022.<br>SWISS is participating in the three-year CICONIA project, which is funded by the EU. Since mid-2023, this project has been looking into what are<br>known as non-CO₂ effects, such as contrails, and how to reduce them. The data sources and IT applications required for climate-optimised flight<br>planning, as well as the related processes for cooperation between the meteorological service, the scientific community and airlines, are being<br>developed and tested in the projects D-KULT and CICONIA, which are coordinated with each other. Since the end of 2023, within the scope of<br>an as yet undefined regulatory framework for non-CO₂ climate impacts such as those related to contrails, the Lufthansa Group has provided its<br>expertise and information to support the German Aerospace Center with the development of a reporting and review system required by the<br>European Commission.
